title: 'Trump's anti-NATO outbursts have undeniably weakened the Atlantic pact'
subhed: By disparaging the North Atlantic Treaty Organization, Trump perhaps aims to assign blame for the United States' setbacks in the Gulf on Europe, columnist Alain Frachon wonders.

title: First non-Iranian oil tanker crosses Strait of Hormuz since ceasefire
Iranians express bitterness at war: 'It's all been destruction, and nothing has truly changed'
Russia and Ukraine agree to Orthodox Easter ceasefire
subhed: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky had proposed a temporary truce earlier this week. Putin has instructed the Russian General Staff 'to cease combat operations in all directions for this period,' the Kremlin said.

title: Giorgia Meloni and the defeat of ideology
subhed: The failure of Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni in the March 22 and 23 referendum on judicial reform marks a lost ideological battle against the founding principles of the Italian Republic, which emerged from the anti-fascist resistance.

title: In Budapest, a seven-hour concert aims to hasten Viktor Orban's 'end of the regime'
subhed: Robert Puzser, an intellectual and outspoken critic of the Fidesz party led by Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orban, is organizing this historic free show on Friday, April 10, to urge citizens to turn out for Sunday's legislative elections.

title: Denis Charbit: 'The Israeli lawmakers who established the death penalty merely obeyed their own instincts for vengeance'
subhed: The professor at the Open University of Israel looks into the recent law introducing the death penalty for Palestinians convicted of terrorist acts and anti-Israeli attacks. He sees it as a grave moral regression and is concerned about Israel abandoning 'thousands of years of ethics.'

Are Trump's ideologues intellectuals or imposters?
subhed: Curtis Yarvin has become a leading figure in the Neo-Reactionary movement (also known as the Dark Enlightenment), which has now made it to the White House. But can this blogger really be considered an intellectual? Political theorist Arnaud Miranda and sociologist Olivier Alexandre debate the origins and roles of one of the thinkers who has Trump's ear.

title: Mysterious 'knocker' continues haunting elderly women in Brittany town
subhed: For the past three years in the village of Goudelin, someone has taken pleasure in harassing women living alone, often elderly, by pounding on their doors in the middle of the night. Around 30 victims have been identified. Speculation is rampant about the knocker's identity, and the victims are finally being heard.

title: In catacombs, museums, and labs, the delicate heritage of human remains
subhed: The Paris Catacombs reopen to the public on Wednesday, April 8, after five months of renovations. The timing offers a chance to reflect on how human remains are handled in cultural and research institutions. These singular relics pose ethical, technical and legal questions.

title: War in Iran: Trump's elusive victory
